title: Chaku
summary: Chaku (Persian: چكو) is a village in Gavork-e Nalin Rural District of the Central District in Mirabad County, West Azerbaijan province, Iran.
text:
Chaku (Persian: چكو) is a village in Gavork-e Nalin Rural District of the Central District in Mirabad County, West Azerbaijan province, Iran.

Demographics
Population
At the time of the 2006 National Census, the village's population was 342 in 54 households, when it was in Vazineh District of Sardasht County. The following census in 2011 counted 361 people in 79 households. The 2016 census measured the population of the village as 400 people in 107 households.
In 2022, the district was separated from the county in the establishment of Mirabad County and renamed the Central District.
